enrichment.short_description: Bagisto is a free, open-source Laravel e-commerce platform for building online stores, multi-vendor marketplaces, and B2B shops.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bagisto is an open-source Laravel e-commerce platform for marketplaces and B2B stores, with optional managed Bagisto Cloud Hosting.

features.title: Multi-vendor marketplace
features.description: Built-in tools for running a marketplace with multiple sellers on a single storefront.

features.title: B2B and B2C commerce
features.description: Supports both direct-to-consumer storefronts and B2B selling scenarios from the same codebase.

features.title: Headless, API-first architecture
features.description: Exposes an API layer so the storefront can be decoupled from the commerce backend.

features.title: Point of Sale (POS)
features.description: Includes a POS module for selling through physical/in-person channels alongside the online store.

features.title: Omnichannel selling
features.description: Lets merchants sell across website, mobile, and POS channels from one admin.

features.title: Multi-tenant SaaS support
features.description: Can be configured to run as a multi-tenant SaaS deployment for hosting multiple stores.

pros: Free and open source with full source code access
Large GitHub community (26K+ stars)
Built on the widely used Laravel PHP framework, easing customization for Laravel developers
Supports multi-vendor, B2B, and headless use cases out of the box
Optional managed Bagisto Cloud Hosting removes the need to self-host
cons: Self-hosting requires PHP/Laravel server administration skills
Bagisto Cloud Hosting pricing is not published on the public site
Advanced enterprise features typically require paid services from Webkul

faqs.question: Is Bagisto free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bagisto's core platform is open source and free to download and self-host.

faqs.question: What framework is Bagisto built on?
faqs.answer: Bagisto is built on the Laravel PHP framework.

faqs.question: Can Bagisto run a multi-vendor marketplace?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bagisto includes multi-vendor marketplace functionality.

faqs.question: Does Bagisto offer hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Webkul offers Bagisto Cloud Hosting as a managed alternative to self-hosting.

use_cases.title: Multi-vendor marketplaces
use_cases.description: Merchants can launch a marketplace where multiple sellers list and manage their own products.

use_cases.title: B2B storefronts
use_cases.description: Businesses can set up B2B-specific pricing, catalogs, and workflows.

use_cases.title: Headless commerce projects
use_cases.description: Development teams can use Bagisto's API layer to power custom frontends.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bagisto?
paragraphs: Bagisto is an open-source e-commerce platform built on the Laravel PHP framework. It is designed for building online stores, multi-vendor marketplaces, and B2B shops, and can also be deployed in a headless, API-first configuration.
The project is maintained by Webkul and is distributed as free, self-hostable software, with an optional managed Bagisto Cloud Hosting service for merchants who don't want to run their own servers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bagisto is for
paragraphs: Bagisto suits developers and agencies comfortable with Laravel who want a customizable, self-hosted e-commerce foundation, as well as merchants launching multi-vendor marketplaces or B2B storefronts.

enrichment.short_description: OpenCart is a free, open-source PHP shopping cart platform with a marketplace of over 13,000 extensions and themes.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenCart is a free, open-source PHP e-commerce platform with a large marketplace of extensions, themes, and payment gateways.

features.title: Extension marketplace
features.description: Official marketplace offering over 13,000 modules and themes to extend store functionality.

features.title: Multi-language and multi-currency
features.description: Built-in support for running stores in multiple languages and currencies.

features.title: Multiple payment gateways
features.description: Integrates with gateways including PayPal, Authorize.Net, Amazon Payments, and Klarna.

features.title: Order and catalog management
features.description: Core admin tools for managing products, orders, and customers.

features.title: GPL open-source license
features.description: Distributed under the GNU General Public License, free to download, modify, and self-host.

pros: Completely free, open-source core software with no licensing fee
One of the largest e-commerce extension marketplaces (13,000+ listings)
No monthly platform fees for the base software
Long-running project with an active developer community
GPL license permits full source code modification
cons: Requires self-hosting and ongoing PHP/server maintenance
Many advanced features depend on paid third-party extensions and themes
No official managed hosting or cloud plan from OpenCart itself

faqs.question: Is OpenCart free?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenCart's core software is free to download and use under the GNU General Public License.

faqs.question: Does OpenCart charge transaction fees?
faqs.answer: OpenCart itself does not charge monthly platform or transaction fees; costs come from hosting, extensions, and themes.

faqs.question: How many extensions does OpenCart have?
faqs.answer: OpenCart's official marketplace lists over 13,000 extensions and themes.

faqs.question: What language is OpenCart written in?
faqs.answer: OpenCart is written in PHP and supports MySQL, MariaDB, and PostgreSQL databases.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted online stores
use_cases.description: Merchants who want full control over hosting and infrastructure can run OpenCart on their own servers.

use_cases.title: Agency-built storefronts
use_cases.description: Agencies can use OpenCart's extension marketplace to assemble custom stores for clients.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenCart?
paragraphs: OpenCart is a free, open-source PHP shopping cart platform originally revived and rewritten by Daniel Kerr, with its first stable release in 2009. It is self-hosted and distributed under the GNU General Public License.

The platform is supported by a large marketplace of over 13,000 extensions and themes, allowing merchants to add payment gateways, shipping methods, and storefront customizations without core code changes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenCart is for
paragraphs: OpenCart fits merchants and agencies who want a free, self-hosted PHP e-commerce foundation and are willing to manage their own server and select extensions to fill in additional functionality.

enrichment.short_description: Thirty Bees is a free, open-source e-commerce platform forked from PrestaShop 1.6, offering 500+ built-in features.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Thirty Bees is an open-source e-commerce platform forked from PrestaShop 1.6, with over 500 built-in features and an add-on marketplace.

features.title: Flexible checkout options
features.description: Supports both one-page and five-step checkout flows.

features.title: Responsive Bootstrap design
features.description: Storefronts are built on Bootstrap for responsive display across devices.

features.title: Multi-site and multi-currency
features.description: Supports running multiple sites and currencies from one installation.

features.title: Built-in SEO tools
features.description: Includes SEO-oriented configuration options for storefronts.

features.title: PDF invoice generation
features.description: Generates PDF invoices for orders natively.

features.title: PrestaShop 1.6 module compatibility
features.description: Retains compatibility with many modules and themes built for PrestaShop 1.6.

pros: Free and open source
Forked from the stable PrestaShop 1.6 codebase, retaining much of its module/theme ecosystem
Ships with 500+ built-in features
Actively maintained, with a version 1.6 release in November 2024
Emphasizes speed and security in its architecture
cons: Smaller community and ecosystem than PrestaShop or larger platforms
Some modules, themes, and support are paid add-ons via its marketplace
Self-hosting requires ongoing server and PHP maintenance

faqs.question: Is Thirty Bees related to PrestaShop?
faqs.answer: Yes, Thirty Bees is an open-source fork of PrestaShop 1.6, created in 2017 to maintain a stable, compatible alternative.

faqs.question: Is Thirty Bees free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core platform is free and open source; paid modules, themes, and support are available through its marketplace.

faqs.question: Is Thirty Bees still maintained?
faqs.answer: Yes, the project released version 1.6 in November 2024, indicating ongoing development.

faqs.question: Can I use PrestaShop 1.6 modules with Thirty Bees?
faqs.answer: Thirty Bees is designed to remain compatible with many PrestaShop 1.6 modules and themes.

use_cases.title: Migrating off PrestaShop 1.6
use_cases.description: Merchants on PrestaShop 1.6 who want continued stability can migrate using Thirty Bees' built-in migration tooling.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted online stores
use_cases.description: Merchants who want a free, self-hosted store with a large built-in feature set.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Thirty Bees?
paragraphs: Thirty Bees is an open-source e-commerce platform that began in 2017 as a fork of PrestaShop 1.6, created by developers who wanted to keep building on that stable codebase after PrestaShop moved to a less backward-compatible 1.7 release.

The platform ships with over 500 built-in features and remains compatible with many PrestaShop 1.6 modules and themes, while continuing its own independent development, including a version 1.6 release in November 2024.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Thirty Bees is for
paragraphs: Thirty Bees is aimed at merchants and developers who used PrestaShop 1.6 and want a compatible, actively maintained, free open-source alternative.

enrichment.short_description: Spree Commerce is an open-source, headless Ruby on Rails e-commerce platform with a Next.js storefront and an optional Enterprise support tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spree Commerce is an open-source, headless e-commerce platform built on Ruby on Rails with a Next.js storefront and enterprise support option.

features.title: REST API and TypeScript SDK
features.description: Exposes REST APIs and a TypeScript SDK for building custom storefronts.

features.title: Next.js storefront
features.description: Ships with a production-ready Next.js storefront that connects to the Rails backend.

features.title: B2B wholesale portals
features.description: Supports custom price lists and approval workflows for B2B buyers.

features.title: Multi-vendor marketplace support
features.description: Can be configured to run multi-vendor marketplace scenarios.

features.title: Multi-tenant architecture
features.description: Supports multi-tenant deployments for embedding commerce into SaaS products.

features.title: Payment gateway abstraction
features.description: Integrates with gateways including Stripe, Adyen, and PayPal.

features.title: Configurable promotions engine
features.description: Includes a pricing and promotions engine for discounts and campaigns.

pros: BSD 3-Clause open-source license with full source code access
No GMV cuts or per-transaction platform fees on the Community Edition
Large community: 15K+ GitHub stars and 2M+ downloads
Used by notable brands including Bookshop, Bonobos, and GoDaddy
Headless architecture with a ready-made Next.js storefront
cons: Requires Ruby on Rails development expertise to self-host and customize
Enterprise Edition pricing is not published publicly
Setting up the Next.js storefront adds implementation complexity beyond the API backend

faqs.question: Is Spree Commerce free?
faqs.answer: The Community Edition is free and open source under a BSD 3-Clause license; an Enterprise Edition with dedicated support is available separately.

faqs.question: What tech stack does Spree use?
faqs.answer: Spree's backend is built on Ruby on Rails, with a Next.js storefront and TypeScript SDKs for integration.

faqs.question: Does Spree charge transaction fees?
faqs.answer: Spree states the Community Edition has no GMV cuts or transaction fees.

faqs.question: Who uses Spree Commerce?
faqs.answer: Spree lists customers including Bookshop, Bonobos, GoDaddy, and KFC among its adopters.

use_cases.title: Headless B2C storefronts
use_cases.description: Teams can build custom storefronts against Spree's REST API and TypeScript SDK, or use its Next.js starter.

use_cases.title: B2B wholesale portals
use_cases.description: Businesses can run B2B ordering with custom price lists and approval workflows.

use_cases.title: Embedded multi-tenant commerce
use_cases.description: SaaS products can embed Spree's multi-tenant commerce engine.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Spree Commerce?
paragraphs: Spree Commerce is an open-source, headless e-commerce platform with a Ruby on Rails backend, a REST API, a TypeScript SDK, and a production-ready Next.js storefront. The Community Edition is BSD 3-Clause licensed and free to self-host.
For businesses that need dedicated support, Spree also offers an Enterprise Edition. The project reports 15K+ GitHub stars, 2M+ downloads, and use by brands such as Bookshop, Bonobos, and GoDaddy.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Spree Commerce is for
paragraphs: Spree suits developers and enterprises building headless, API-driven commerce, including B2B wholesale, multi-vendor marketplace, and multi-tenant SaaS scenarios, who are comfortable with a Ruby on Rails backend.

enrichment.short_description: Solidus is a free, open-source Ruby on Rails e-commerce framework forked from Spree in 2015, maintained by Nebulab and the community.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Solidus is an open-source Ruby on Rails e-commerce framework forked from Spree, with an optional fully hosted Solidus Cloud Enterprise plan.

features.title: Merchandising and personalization
features.description: Supports personalization using first-party data, upsells, and content integration.

features.title: Native subscriptions engine
features.description: Includes a subscription engine for curated boxes and recurring purchase models.

features.title: Custom promotions logic
features.description: Allows custom promotion rules beyond simple discount codes.

features.title: Internationalization
features.description: Supports multi-language and localization for global storefronts.

features.title: REST and GraphQL APIs
features.description: Provides both REST and GraphQL APIs for building custom frontends and integrations.

features.title: Starter theme and admin dashboard
features.description: Ships with a starter storefront theme and an admin dashboard for store management.

pros: Free and open source with full source code access
Highly customizable Ruby on Rails framework aimed at complex/custom commerce needs
Native subscriptions support built in
Both REST and GraphQL APIs available
Maintained by Nebulab and an active open-source community
cons: Requires Ruby on Rails development expertise to implement and customize
Solidus Cloud (Enterprise) pricing is not publicly listed
Smaller ecosystem than larger hosted platforms

faqs.question: Is Solidus free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidus is a free, open-source framework that can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: What is Solidus built on?
faqs.answer: Solidus is built on Ruby on Rails and was forked from Spree in 2015.

faqs.question: Does Solidus offer managed hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidus Cloud is offered as a fully hosted Enterprise option with dedicated support.

faqs.question: Who maintains Solidus?
faqs.answer: Solidus is maintained by Nebulab and the open-source community.

use_cases.title: Custom-built brand storefronts
use_cases.description: DTC and CPG brands needing deep customization beyond standard platforms can build on Solidus.

use_cases.title: Subscription commerce
use_cases.description: Merchants running curated-box or recurring subscription models can use Solidus' native subscriptions engine.

use_cases.title: Fully hosted enterprise commerce
use_cases.description: Larger organizations can use Solidus Cloud for a fully hosted deployment with dedicated support.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Solidus?
paragraphs: Solidus is an open-source e-commerce framework built on Ruby on Rails. It was created in 2015 as a fork of Spree by the team behind the Bonobos store, after Spree's development direction changed, and it is now maintained by Nebulab and the open-source community.

Solidus targets brands that need deep customization, offering merchandising/personalization tools, a native subscriptions engine, custom promotions logic, and both REST and GraphQL APIs. A fully hosted Solidus Cloud Enterprise option is also available.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Solidus is for
paragraphs: Solidus is aimed at DTC, CPG, fashion, subscription, and marketplace brands that need custom commerce logic beyond what standard SaaS platforms offer, and have (or can hire) Ruby on Rails development capacity.

enrichment.short_description: Drupal Commerce is a free, open-source e-commerce module for the Drupal CMS, maintained by Centarro since 2010.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Drupal Commerce is a free, open-source e-commerce module that integrates content, product, and order management within the Drupal CMS.

features.title: Multi-site, multi-language, multi-currency
features.description: Supports running multiple sites, languages, and currencies from a Drupal installation.

features.title: Advanced product display pages
features.description: Leverages Drupal's media library and layout builder for product pages.

features.title: Search API integration
features.description: Integrates with Drupal's Search API for keyword search and faceted filtering.

features.title: Promotion and coupon management
features.description: Includes tools for configuring promotions and coupon codes.

features.title: Custom fields and layouts
features.description: Merchants can configure custom fields and page layouts using Drupal's field and layout systems.

features.title: Third-party API integrations
features.description: Integrates with external applications via APIs and Drupal's event system.

pros: Free and open source
Deep integration with Drupal's content management and layout tools
Actively maintained, with Commerce Core 3.2.0 released in September 2025
Flexible custom field and layout configuration for merchandising
Paid consulting and support available through maintainer Centarro
cons: Requires Drupal CMS knowledge to implement and maintain
Smaller dedicated e-commerce community compared to standalone commerce platforms
Self-hosting and Drupal maintenance are the merchant's responsibility

faqs.question: Is Drupal Commerce free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Drupal Commerce is free, open-source software; paid consulting and development services are available separately through Centarro.

faqs.question: What platform does Drupal Commerce run on?
faqs.answer: Drupal Commerce is a module that runs on top of the Drupal content management system.

faqs.question: Who maintains Drupal Commerce?
faqs.answer: Drupal Commerce is maintained by Centarro, which has been involved with the project since its creation in 2010.

faqs.question: What was the latest Drupal Commerce release?
faqs.answer: Commerce Core 3.2.0 was released on September 10, 2025.

use_cases.title: Content-driven commerce sites
use_cases.description: Organizations that already run Drupal for content can add commerce without switching platforms.

use_cases.title: Multi-site, multi-language stores
use_cases.description: Businesses needing multiple storefronts in different languages/currencies from one codebase.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Drupal Commerce?
paragraphs: Drupal Commerce is a free, open-source e-commerce module for the Drupal content management system, maintained by Centarro since the project's creation in 2010. It integrates product, content, and order management within a single Drupal site.

The platform supports multi-site, multi-language, and multi-currency stores, and takes advantage of Drupal's media library, layout builder, and Search API for product display and search. Centarro also offers paid consulting and development services.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Drupal Commerce is for
paragraphs: Drupal Commerce fits agencies and organizations already building on Drupal who want to add e-commerce capability using the same CMS, content, and layout tooling they already use.

enrichment.short_description: GrandNode is a free, open-source ASP.NET Core e-commerce platform released under the GPL-3 license, with optional paid support packages.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GrandNode is a free, open-source ASP.NET Core e-commerce platform under GPL-3, supporting B2B, B2C, marketplace, and headless commerce.

features.title: B2C and B2B store management
features.description: Supports customer grouping and custom pricing for both B2C and B2B selling.

features.title: Multi-vendor marketplace
features.description: Includes marketplace functionality for running multi-vendor storefronts.

features.title: Cross-channel integration
features.description: Integrates with channels such as Amazon and eBay, including inventory sync.

features.title: Booking and reservation system
features.description: Supports booking/reservation-based products such as accommodations and services.

features.title: E-learning and digital products
features.description: Supports selling digital products and paid courses.

features.title: Headless commerce API
features.description: Provides an API architecture for headless commerce implementations.

pros: Free and open source under the GPL-3 license
Built on the modern ASP.NET Core / .NET stack
Covers multiple business models (B2B, marketplace, bookings, digital products) in one platform
Headless API architecture for custom frontends
Optional premium support packages available for teams that want vendor support
cons: Requires .NET development expertise to self-host and customize
Smaller community than PHP-based open-source e-commerce platforms
Specific premium support pricing is not published on the public site

faqs.question: Is GrandNode free?
faqs.answer: Yes, GrandNode's core platform is free and open source under the GPL-3 license.

faqs.question: What technology is GrandNode built on?
faqs.answer: GrandNode is built on ASP.NET Core (.NET).

faqs.question: Does GrandNode support B2B selling?
faqs.answer: Yes, GrandNode supports B2B store management including customer grouping and custom pricing.

faqs.question: Is paid support available for GrandNode?
faqs.answer: Yes, GrandNode offers premium support packages and custom services in addition to its free open-source core.

use_cases.title: Multi-model commerce on .NET
use_cases.description: Teams standardized on .NET can run B2B, B2C, marketplace, or booking-based commerce from one platform.

use_cases.title: Headless commerce backends
use_cases.description: Developers can use GrandNode's API to power custom storefronts.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GrandNode?
paragraphs: GrandNode is a free, open-source e-commerce platform built on ASP.NET Core and released under the GPL-3 license. It is designed to support multiple business models, including B2B, B2C, multi-vendor marketplace, bookings/reservations, and digital products/e-learning.
The core platform is self-hosted and free to use, with optional premium support packages and custom implementation services available for teams that need vendor support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GrandNode is for
paragraphs: GrandNode fits developers and agencies working in the .NET ecosystem who want a single open-source platform capable of handling B2B, marketplace, booking, or digital-product commerce.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: nopCommerce is a free, open-source ASP.NET Core e-commerce platform released under the nopCommerce Public License, with optional paid support.

features.title: ASP.NET Core architecture

features.title: Multi-store support
features.description: Supports running multiple stores from a single installation.

features.description: Offers a marketplace of plugins and themes to extend store functionality.

features.description: Includes tools for configuring discounts and promotional campaigns.

features.title: Multi-currency and multi-language
features.description: Supports selling in multiple currencies and languages.

pros: Free to download and use commercially, with no license fee
Long-running project first released in 2008, with 3M+ downloads
Actively developed, with version 4.90.6 released in July 2026
Extensive plugin and theme marketplace
Optional premium support available for teams that need it
cons: Requires ASP.NET Core / .NET hosting expertise to self-host
Many themes and plugins in the marketplace are paid add-ons
Distributed under a custom nopCommerce Public License rather than a standard OSI license

faqs.question: Is nopCommerce free?
faqs.answer: Yes, nopCommerce is free to download, install, and use commercially, released under the nopCommerce Public License.

faqs.question: What technology does nopCommerce use?
faqs.answer: nopCommerce is built on ASP.NET Core with an MS SQL Server backend.

faqs.question: How long has nopCommerce been around?
faqs.answer: Development started in 2008, and the platform has been downloaded more than 3 million times.

faqs.question: Does nopCommerce offer paid support?
faqs.answer: Yes, optional premium support and paid plugins/themes are available alongside the free core platform.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted .NET storefronts
use_cases.description: Merchants and agencies standardized on Microsoft/.NET infrastructure can run nopCommerce as their store platform.

use_cases.title: Multi-store retail operations
use_cases.description: Businesses running several storefronts can manage them from a single nopCommerce installation.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is nopCommerce?
paragraphs: nopCommerce is a free, open-source e-commerce platform built on ASP.NET Core with an MS SQL Server backend. Development began in 2008, and the platform has since been downloaded more than 3 million times.

The software is distributed under the nopCommerce Public License and is free to download and use commercially. An ecosystem of plugins, themes, and optional premium support is available for merchants who want additional functionality or vendor assistance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who nopCommerce is for
paragraphs: nopCommerce suits merchants and development teams working within a Microsoft/.NET technology stack who want a free, self-hosted, actively maintained e-commerce platform.

enrichment.short_description: Snipcart is a drop-in shopping cart and checkout platform for any website, priced at 2% per transaction with a $20/month minimum.
enrichment.starting_price: 2% per transaction (minimum $20/month for stores under $1,000 USD in monthly sales)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Snipcart is a drop-in shopping cart and checkout platform for any website, charging 2% per transaction with a free testing environment.

features.title: Drop-in cart snippet
features.description: Add a shopping cart and checkout flow to an existing site with an HTML/JS snippet, no redirect required.

features.title: Physical and digital products
features.description: Supports selling both physical goods and digital products.

features.title: Abandoned cart recovery
features.description: Includes tools to recover abandoned shopping carts.

features.title: Multi-currency and tax management
features.description: Handles multiple currencies and tax calculation for orders.

features.title: Multiple payment gateways
features.description: Integrates with several payment gateways for checkout.

features.title: API and webhooks
features.description: Provides an API and webhooks for custom integrations.

pros: Works with virtually any tech stack, including Jamstack, React, Vue, WordPress, and Laravel sites
Free, unlimited testing environment with no credit card required
Pure usage-based pricing with no separate platform subscription fee
Unlimited products, variants, and domains on the single plan
Built-in abandoned cart recovery and tax handling
cons: 2% transaction fee applies on top of payment gateway fees
$20/month minimum fee for stores under $1,000 in monthly sales
Requires developer integration into an existing site rather than providing a full storefront builder

faqs.question: How much does Snipcart cost?
faqs.answer: Snipcart charges 2% per transaction, with a $20/month minimum fee for stores generating under $1,000 USD in monthly sales.

faqs.question: Is Snipcart free to test?
faqs.answer: Yes, Snipcart's testing/development environment is free with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What platforms does Snipcart work with?
faqs.answer: Snipcart is designed to work with any tech stack, including Jamstack, React, Vue, WordPress, and Laravel.

faqs.question: Can Snipcart sell digital products?
faqs.answer: Yes, Snipcart supports selling both physical and digital products.

use_cases.title: Adding a cart to a custom-built site
use_cases.description: Developers can add checkout functionality to a static or custom site without building a cart from scratch.

use_cases.title: Selling on a Jamstack or headless site
use_cases.description: Sites built with static site generators or headless CMSs can add commerce via Snipcart's drop-in cart.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Snipcart?
paragraphs: Snipcart is a shopping cart and checkout platform that developers add to an existing website via an HTML/JS snippet, rather than requiring a full storefront rebuild. It supports physical and digital products, abandoned cart recovery, multi-currency and tax handling, and multiple payment gateways.

Pricing is usage-based: Snipcart charges 2% per transaction, with a $20/month minimum for stores under $1,000 in monthly sales, and offers a free, unlimited testing environment with no credit card required.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Snipcart is for
paragraphs: Snipcart is built for developers and agencies who want to bolt e-commerce functionality onto an existing site — including Jamstack, React, Vue, WordPress, and Laravel builds — without adopting a full standalone e-commerce platform.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source subscription billing and payments platform you self-host for full data control.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kill Bill is a free, open-source (Apache 2.0) billing platform for subscription, usage-based, and multi-tenant billing you self-host.

features.title: Subscription billing engine
features.description: Supports usage-based, prepaid, postpaid, and multi-tenant subscription models.

features.title: Payment gateway plugins
features.description: Native plugin support for Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, Braintree, and other gateways.

features.title: Kaui admin UI
features.description: A back-office web interface for managing subscriptions and payments.

features.title: Plugin architecture
features.description: An extensible framework for building custom integrations and modifications.

features.title: Tax engine integrations
features.description: Connects to tax and compliance engines such as Avalara and Vertex.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Deploys via Docker or Kubernetes in your own infrastructure, avoiding SaaS lock-in.

pros: Core platform is free and fully open source under Apache 2.0
Full data ownership since it runs in your own infrastructure
No per-transaction or revenue-percentage fees
Long track record, in production for over 15 years
Plugin architecture supports many payment gateways and tax engines
cons: Requires engineering resources to deploy, operate, and maintain
No official low-code hosted option from the core project itself (managed hosting is via third parties)
Enterprise accelerator (Aviate) is a separate proprietary offering, not part of the free core

faqs.question: Is Kill Bill free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Kill Bill platform is open source under the Apache License 2.0 and free to self-host.

faqs.question: Do I need to host Kill Bill myself?
faqs.answer: Kill Bill is designed to be self-hosted via Docker or Kubernetes; third-party providers such as ChaChing offer managed hosting.

faqs.question: What payment gateways does Kill Bill support?
faqs.answer: It has native plugin support for Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, Braintree, and other gateways.

faqs.question: What language is Kill Bill written in?

long_form_blocks.heading: What is NetBox?
paragraphs: NetBox is an open-source network source-of-truth platform used to model and document data center infrastructure (DCIM) and IP address space (IPAM). It stores network device, rack, cabling, IP, VLAN, and circuit data in a central database that automation tools can query.

The core project is Apache 2.0 licensed and free to self-host from github.com/netbox-community/netbox. NetBox Labs also offers commercially hosted (NetBox Cloud) and self-hosted, supported (NetBox Enterprise) editions on top of the open-source core.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who NetBox is for
paragraphs: NetBox suits network engineers and infrastructure teams that need a structured, queryable record of their network as the foundation for automation, rather than spreadsheets or disconnected tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ActivityWatch?

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SeedDMS?
paragraphs: SeedDMS is a free, open-source document management system built on PHP that organizes documents and digital media in a hierarchical folder structure. It supports full-text search, document preview and format conversion, LDAP authentication, WebDAV access, and OAI-PMH metadata harvesting.
The project has been developed for over a decade and is distributed under the GNU General Public License version 2 (GPLv2), with source code available for self-hosted deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SeedDMS is for
paragraphs: SeedDMS is suited to small and medium-sized organizations that want a free, self-hosted document management system and are comfortable running and maintaining a PHP-based web application.

paragraphs: ownCloud is an open-source platform for secure file sharing, syncing, and real-time content collaboration. long_form_blocks.heading: What is Peergos?
paragraphs: Peergos is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted cloud storage and file-sharing platform that uses post-quantum encryption to protect files in transit and at rest, and hides metadata such as contact lists, file sizes, and directory structures. It is available for Windows, macOS, Linux, and Android.

The project is open source under AGPL-3.0, with code published on GitHub, and has undergone independent security audits by Cure53 and Radically Open Security. Users can self-host Peergos for free or subscribe to hosted plans starting at £3/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Peergos is for
paragraphs: Peergos is aimed at privacy-focused individuals who want end-to-end encrypted cloud storage, and at technical users who prefer to self-host their own encrypted storage server for free while still being able to connect with users on other Peergos servers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Filestash?
paragraphs: Filestash is a self-hosted file management platform that provides a unified interface for browsing, organizing, and sharing files across 30+ storage backends, including S3, SFTP, FTP, WebDAV, SMB, NFS, Git, and SharePoint. It supports SSO via LDAP, SAML, and OIDC, role-based access control with AWS IAM support, and AI features such as semantic search, smart folders, and OCR with Ollama support.

The free, open-source Hobby tier (AGPLv3) supports self-hosting for up to 3 users, while paid Self Served and Enterprise plans add commercial licensing, SSO/RBAC, white-labeling, audit and compliance logging, and 24/7 support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Filestash is for

faqs.answer: Yes, FileBrowser is free, open-source software licensed under Apache-2.0. There is no paid tier.

faqs.answer: Yes, but it is in maintenance-only mode: the maintainer fixes bugs and security issues but is not actively adding new features.

faqs.answer: FileBrowser ships as a single self-contained binary and also has official Docker images, so it can be installed on your own server or run as a container.

faqs.answer: Yes, it includes a user management system with individual logins and permission scopes.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is FileBrowser?
paragraphs: FileBrowser is an open-source web file manager. You install it on a server, point it at a directory, and it provides a browser-based interface for uploading, previewing, editing, and organizing files.

It ships as a single self-contained binary and has official Docker images, so it can be self-hosted without additional server infrastructure beyond the machine running it. The project is currently in maintenance-only mode: its creator continues to fix bugs and security issues but is not building new features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who FileBrowser is for
paragraphs: FileBrowser suits self-hosters and small teams who want a lightweight, no-cost web interface for managing files on their own servers, rather than relying on a third-party cloud storage provider.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted S3-compatible storage
use_cases.description: Run an S3-compatible object store on your own infrastructure instead of using a public cloud storage provider.

features: Lite SUBNET subscription with health diagnostics and security reporting
Optional engineer support add-on with under-5-day response SLA
Enterprise SUBNET subscription with team governance

long_form_blocks.heading: What is MinIO?
paragraphs: MinIO is an S3-compatible object storage system built for self-hosted deployment across edge, on-premises, and cloud environments. It is now positioned by its maker as MinIO AIStor, targeting AI, analytics, and data lakehouse workloads with support for formats such as Apache Iceberg.

MinIO offers a free, full-featured single-node deployment, while multi-node, higher-capacity deployments require a paid Enterprise Lite or Enterprise subscription with custom pricing. Notably, the original community open-source repository was archived in 2026 as the project shifted its primary development focus to the commercial AIStor product line.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who MinIO is for
paragraphs: MinIO suits teams that need S3-compatible object storage they control on their own infrastructure, from individual developers running a free single-node instance to enterprises running multi-node, high-throughput deployments under a paid support contract.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, self-hosted S3-compatible object storage system built for reliability across geographically distributed, low-resource hardware.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Garage is a free, open-source S3-compatible object storage system designed to run reliably on distributed, low-power hardware.

features.title: S3 API compatibility
features.description: Exposes an S3-compatible API, allowing tools such as Nextcloud, Matrix, Mastodon, Rclone, PeerTube, and Cyberduck to use Garage as a storage backend.

features.title: Multi-zone data replication
features.description: Each chunk of data is replicated across three zones to protect against hardware or site failure.

features.title: Low resource requirements
features.description: Runs with as little as 1GB of RAM and 16GB of disk space, and supports CPUs over ten years old as well as ARMv7/ARMv8 hardware.

features.title: Single-binary deployment
features.description: Ships as a self-contained, dependency-free executable that runs across Linux distributions without external services.

features.title: Wide-area network resilience
features.description: Designed to operate over the internet across multiple datacenters with heterogeneous, non-datacenter-grade hardware.

pros: Completely free and open source (AGPL-3.0 license)
Very low hardware requirements make it viable on old or low-power machines
S3-compatible API works with many existing self-hosted apps
Built specifically for resilience across geographically distributed, unreliable networks
cons: No managed/hosted cloud offering — deployment and operation are entirely self-managed
Smaller community and ecosystem than larger object storage projects like MinIO or Ceph
Documentation and tooling are comparatively lean, reflecting its focus on a lightweight core

faqs.question: Is Garage free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Garage is free, open-source software licensed under AGPL-3.0.

faqs.question: Is Garage S3-compatible?
faqs.answer: Yes, Garage implements an S3-compatible API, so it works with tools like Nextcloud, Rclone, and Cyberduck.

faqs.question: What hardware does Garage need?
faqs.answer: Garage is designed to run on minimal hardware, including CPUs more than 10 years old and ARM devices, with as little as 1GB RAM and 16GB of disk space.

faqs.question: Who maintains Garage?
faqs.answer: Garage is developed by Deuxfleurs, a project that has received public funding through European initiatives including NGI POINTER, NLnet/NGI0 Entrust, and the NGI0 Commons Fund.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted object storage for small clusters
use_cases.description: Provide S3-compatible storage for self-hosted applications on modest or repurposed hardware distributed across multiple sites.

use_cases.title: Backend storage for federated services
use_cases.description: Serve as the storage backend for federated or open-source platforms such as Nextcloud, Matrix, Mastodon, and PeerTube.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Garage?
paragraphs: Garage is an open-source, S3-compatible object storage system built to run reliably across distributed infrastructure, including hardware outside traditional datacenters. It replicates each chunk of data across three zones and is designed to tolerate heterogeneous, low-power hardware.
It ships as a single self-contained binary with minimal resource requirements, and is developed by Deuxfleurs with public funding from European digital infrastructure initiatives.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Garage is for
paragraphs: Garage fits self-hosters and small teams who want S3-compatible object storage that can run on modest, geographically distributed, or older hardware, particularly as a backend for federated open-source applications.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, self-hosted unified storage platform combining object, block, and file storage in a single distributed system.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ceph is a free, open-source unified storage platform offering S3/Swift-compatible object storage, block devices, and a POSIX file system.

features.title: Object storage
features.description: Provides a RESTful gateway with S3- and Swift-compatible APIs, along with user management and multi-site deployment support.

features.title: Block storage (RBD)
features.description: Offers thin-provisioned block devices with images up to 16 exabytes, snapshots, and copy-on-write cloning.

features.title: File system (CephFS)
features.description: A POSIX-compliant distributed file system accessible via kernel client or FUSE, with Hadoop integration support.

features.title: Multisite replication
features.description: Supports disaster recovery through multisite replication for both object and block storage.

features.title: Community governance
features.description: Developed under the non-profit Ceph Foundation with a large, active open-source community.

pros: Free and open source, licensed under LGPL
Unifies object, block, and file storage in one platform instead of requiring separate systems
S3- and Swift-compatible object storage API
Backed by an active community and the non-profit Ceph Foundation
cons: No official managed cloud service from the Ceph project itself — deployment and operations are self-managed
Operating a Ceph cluster reliably at scale requires significant infrastructure and operational expertise
No published pricing since it is community software rather than a commercial product

faqs.question: Is Ceph free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ceph is free, open-source software licensed under LGPL, developed under the non-profit Ceph Foundation.

faqs.question: What kinds of storage does Ceph provide?
faqs.answer: Ceph provides object storage (S3/Swift-compatible), block storage (RBD), and a POSIX file system (CephFS) from a single unified system.

faqs.question: Can Ceph be used as an S3-compatible store?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ceph's object storage gateway exposes an S3- and Swift-compliant REST API.

faqs.question: Does Ceph support disaster recovery?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ceph supports multisite replication for disaster recovery across both object and block storage.

use_cases.title: Unified storage backend for private clouds
use_cases.description: Provide object, block, and file storage from a single distributed cluster for private cloud or on-premises infrastructure.

use_cases.title: S3-compatible storage at scale
use_cases.description: Run a self-hosted, S3-compatible object store for applications that need large-scale, replicated storage without a public cloud provider.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ceph?
paragraphs: Ceph is a free, open-source distributed storage platform that unifies object, block, and file storage in one system. Its object storage gateway offers S3- and Swift-compatible APIs, its RBD component provides thin-provisioned block devices with snapshots and cloning, and CephFS offers a POSIX-compliant distributed file system.
Ceph is developed under the non-profit Ceph Foundation and is licensed under LGPL, making it fully free and self-hostable on your own infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ceph is for
paragraphs: Ceph suits infrastructure and platform teams that need a single, self-hosted storage system to serve object, block, and file storage needs at scale, typically for private clouds or large on-premises deployments.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source distributed storage system for billions of files, with S3, Iceberg table, and POSIX filesystem support, plus a paid Enterprise edition.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SeaweedFS is a free, open-source distributed storage system for billions of files, with an Enterprise edition priced at $2 per TB per month.

features.title: Unified S3, Iceberg, and POSIX access
features.description: Serves S3 objects, Apache Iceberg tables, and POSIX files from a single storage cluster.

features.title: Point-in-time recovery
features.description: Enterprise edition allows rolling back folders, buckets, or objects to a specific moment in time.

features.title: Customizable erasure coding
features.description: Tunable erasure coding ratios (e.g., 20+4) reduce storage overhead to roughly 1.2x versus 3x for traditional replication.

features.title: Self-healing storage
features.description: Automatically detects and removes corrupted data, with automatic erasure-coding shard repair.

features.title: Kernel mount
features.description: Provides a native Linux filesystem mount with flat memory usage regardless of file count.

features.title: Admin UI with OIDC
features.description: Includes an administration interface with OIDC identity-provider integration and multi-tenant S3 quality-of-service rate limiting (Enterprise).

pros: Free, open-source core under the Apache-2.0 license with no feature gates for development/testing under 25TB
Enterprise pricing is transparently published ($2/TB per month or $20/TB per year) rather than requiring a sales quote
Enterprise features apply via a license key with no migration or architecture change needed
Handles object, table (Iceberg), and POSIX file storage from one cluster
cons: Advanced features like point-in-time recovery, self-healing, and multi-tenancy require a paid Enterprise license
Free tier is explicitly scoped to development and testing under 25TB
Self-hosted only — no managed cloud service is offered

faqs.question: Is SeaweedFS free?
faqs.answer: The open-source core is free with no feature gates for development and testing under 25TB. Production use of Enterprise features requires a paid license.

faqs.question: How much does SeaweedFS Enterprise cost?
faqs.answer: SeaweedFS Enterprise is priced at $2 per TB per month, or $20 per TB per year.

faqs.question: Does SeaweedFS support S3?
faqs.answer: Yes, SeaweedFS can serve S3-compatible objects, alongside Apache Iceberg tables and POSIX files, from the same cluster.

faqs.question: Can I upgrade an existing SeaweedFS cluster to Enterprise?
faqs.answer: Yes, Enterprise features are enabled by applying a license key to an existing SeaweedFS deployment, without requiring migration or a new architecture.

use_cases.title: Large-scale file and object storage
use_cases.description: Store and serve billions of files or S3 objects from a self-hosted distributed cluster.
use_cases.audience: Infrastructure teams handling large file volumes

use_cases.title: Data lakehouse storage backend
use_cases.description: Serve Apache Iceberg tables alongside object and file storage from a single SeaweedFS cluster for analytics workloads.

features: Intended for development and testing under 25TB
Self-healing storage with automatic erasure-coding repair
Multi-tenancy with S3 QoS rate limiting

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SeaweedFS?
paragraphs: SeaweedFS is an open-source distributed storage system built to store and serve billions of files quickly. It can serve S3-compatible objects, Apache Iceberg tables, and POSIX-compliant files from a single cluster.

The open-source core, licensed under Apache-2.0, has no feature gates for development and testing under 25TB. A separate Enterprise edition, priced at $2 per TB per month (or $20 per TB per year), adds features like point-in-time recovery, self-healing storage, and multi-tenancy via a license key applied to an existing deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SeaweedFS is for
paragraphs: SeaweedFS suits teams that need to self-host storage for very large numbers of files or objects, from developers testing on the free tier to organizations running production clusters under a paid Enterprise license for advanced data protection features.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source distributed network filesystem for scalable storage across standard hardware, sponsored by Red Hat.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GlusterFS is a free, open-source distributed network filesystem for scalable storage on commodity hardware.

features.title: Distributed network filesystem
features.description: Aggregates storage from multiple servers into a single scalable network filesystem using standard hardware.

features.title: Scales on commodity hardware
features.description: Designed to run on standard, non-proprietary servers rather than requiring specialized storage appliances.

features.title: Suited to bandwidth-intensive workloads
features.description: Used for use cases such as media streaming and data analysis that require high throughput.

features.title: Active documentation and release process
features.description: Maintains ongoing documentation, a public roadmap, and a defined release schedule.

pros: Free and open source, licensed under GPL-2.0
Runs on standard commodity hardware without proprietary storage appliances
Long-established project with mature documentation and installation/administration guides
Development continues with community maintainers after Red Hat's commercial product wind-down
cons: Red Hat ended commercial support for its Red Hat Gluster Storage product at the end of 2024
Development pace has slowed since Red Hat's withdrawal from the commercial side of the project
No managed cloud offering — GlusterFS is entirely self-hosted and self-managed

faqs.question: Is GlusterFS free?
faqs.answer: Yes, GlusterFS is free, open-source software licensed under GPL-2.0.

faqs.question: Is GlusterFS still maintained?
faqs.answer: Yes. Although Red Hat discontinued its commercial Red Hat Gluster Storage product at the end of 2024, the open-source GlusterFS project continues to receive maintainer-driven updates and releases.

faqs.question: What is GlusterFS used for?
faqs.answer: GlusterFS is used to build distributed, scalable network filesystems on commodity hardware, including for bandwidth-intensive workloads like media streaming and data analysis.

faqs.question: Who backs GlusterFS?
faqs.answer: GlusterFS is copyrighted by Red Hat, Inc. and developed as an open-source project with an active community and public GitHub repositories.

use_cases.title: Scalable storage on commodity servers
use_cases.description: Aggregate standard servers into a distributed filesystem for scalable storage without specialized hardware.

use_cases.title: Bandwidth-intensive workloads
use_cases.description: Support media streaming and large-scale data analysis workloads that require high-throughput distributed storage.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GlusterFS?
paragraphs: GlusterFS is a free and open-source scalable network filesystem that aggregates storage from standard commodity servers into a single distributed filesystem. It is commonly used for bandwidth-intensive workloads such as media streaming and data analysis.

The project is copyrighted by Red Hat, Inc. and licensed under GPL-2.0. Red Hat ended commercial support for its own Red Hat Gluster Storage product at the end of 2024, but the underlying open-source GlusterFS project continues to be maintained by the community.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GlusterFS is for
paragraphs: GlusterFS fits infrastructure teams that want to build distributed, scalable storage from their own commodity hardware without a commercial support contract, particularly for high-throughput workloads.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source backup program that encrypts and deduplicates data, backing up to many storage backends from a single executable.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Restic is a free, open-source backup program that only transfers changed data, encrypts backups, and supports many storage backends.

features.title: Incremental backups
features.description: Only transfers the parts of files that actually changed since the last backup, reducing bandwidth and storage use.

features.title: Single executable
features.description: Ships as a single binary requiring no separate server infrastructure to run.

features.title: Multiple storage backends
features.description: Backs up to a wide range of self-hosted and cloud storage services.

features.title: Backup verification
features.description: Includes built-in checks to confirm that backups can actually be restored.

features.title: Cross-platform support
features.description: Runs on Linux, BSD, macOS, and Windows.

pros: Completely free and open source under the BSD 2-Clause License
Efficient incremental backups that only transfer changed data
Works with many storage backends, both self-hosted and cloud-based
No server component required — runs as a single executable
Maintains backward compatibility following Semantic Versioning
cons: Command-line only — no official graphical interface
No managed/hosted service; you must supply and manage your own storage backend
No commercial support plan offered directly by the project

faqs.question: Is Restic free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Restic is completely free and open source, licensed under the BSD 2-Clause License.

faqs.question: What platforms does Restic support?
faqs.answer: Restic runs on Linux, BSD, macOS, and Windows.

faqs.question: Does Restic encrypt backups?
faqs.answer: Yes, Restic uses modern cryptography to secure backup data.

faqs.question: Does Restic require a server?
faqs.answer: No, Restic is a single executable and does not require any dedicated server infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Encrypted backups to cloud or self-hosted storage
use_cases.description: Back up files with encryption and deduplication to a wide range of storage backends, self-hosted or cloud-based.

use_cases.title: Cross-platform personal and server backups
use_cases.description: Run the same backup tool consistently across Linux, macOS, BSD, and Windows machines.
use_cases.audience: Individuals and small teams managing mixed-OS environments

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Restic?
paragraphs: Restic is a free, open-source backup program that only transfers the parts of files that have changed since the last backup, minimizing bandwidth and storage overhead. It ships as a single executable and requires no dedicated server infrastructure.

Backups are encrypted and can be verified to confirm they can actually be restored. Restic supports a wide range of storage backends, both self-hosted and cloud-based, and runs on Linux, BSD, macOS, and Windows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Restic is for
paragraphs: Restic suits developers and system administrators who want a free, scriptable, cross-platform command-line backup tool that works with many different storage destinations.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source deduplicating backup archiver with authenticated encryption and multiple compression algorithms.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: BorgBackup is a free, open-source deduplicating archiver offering space-efficient, encrypted backups for Linux, macOS, and BSD.

features.title: Data deduplication
features.description: Provides space-efficient storage of backups by deduplicating data across archives.

features.title: Authenticated encryption
features.description: Secures backup archives with authenticated encryption to protect data confidentiality and integrity.

features.title: Multiple compression algorithms
features.description: Supports lz4, zstd, zlib, and lzma compression algorithms for backup archives.

features.title: FUSE mounting
features.description: Backup archives can be mounted with FUSE and browsed like a regular filesystem.

pros: Free and open-source under the BSD license
Space-efficient deduplication reduces storage costs for repeated backups
Strong authenticated encryption built in
Supports several compression algorithms to balance speed and ratio
Active open-source community on GitHub
cons: Command-line only — no official native graphical interface
No managed/hosted backup service directly from the core project (third-party commercial services are listed separately)
Requires users to manage their own storage destination

faqs.question: Is BorgBackup free?
faqs.answer: Yes, BorgBackup is free, open-source software distributed under the BSD license.

faqs.question: What platforms does BorgBackup support?
faqs.answer: BorgBackup supports Linux, macOS, BSD, and other platforms.

faqs.question: Does BorgBackup deduplicate data?
faqs.answer: Yes, deduplication is a core feature, providing space-efficient storage of backups.

faqs.question: Can I browse a BorgBackup archive like a filesystem?
faqs.answer: Yes, archives can be mounted using FUSE, making backed-up data browsable like a regular filesystem.

use_cases.title: Space-efficient encrypted backups
use_cases.description: Create deduplicated, encrypted backup archives for servers or workstations to minimize storage usage.

use_cases.title: Cross-platform Unix-like backups
use_cases.description: Run consistent backup workflows across Linux, macOS, and BSD systems.
use_cases.audience: Individuals and teams managing Unix-like infrastructure

long_form_blocks.heading: What is BorgBackup?
paragraphs: BorgBackup is a free, open-source deduplicating archiver focused on space-efficient, encrypted backup storage. It supports multiple compression algorithms (lz4, zstd, zlib, lzma) and secures archives with authenticated encryption.
Backup archives can be mounted with FUSE and browsed like a normal filesystem. BorgBackup is distributed under the BSD license and supports Linux, macOS, and BSD.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who BorgBackup is for
paragraphs: BorgBackup suits system administrators and developers who want a free, encrypted, deduplicating backup tool for Unix-like systems, without relying on a commercial backup service.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source backup tool that creates encrypted, compressed, and deduplicated backups to cloud or local storage, with both CLI and GUI.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kopia is a free, open-source backup tool offering encrypted, compressed, deduplicated backups with both a desktop GUI and command-line interface.

features.title: Encrypted, compressed, deduplicated backups
features.description: Creates backups that are encrypted, compressed, and deduplicated to reduce storage size while protecting data.

features.title: Pluggable encryption and compression
features.description: Supports configurable encryption and compression options for backup repositories.

features.title: Snapshot and restore
features.description: Creates point-in-time snapshots and supports restoring individual files or full snapshots.

features.title: Policy-based backup rules
features.description: Lets users define policies controlling how and when backups are taken and retained.

features.title: Desktop and command-line interfaces
features.description: Provides both a graphical desktop application and a command-line interface for scripting and automation.

pros: Free and open source, hosted on GitHub
Offers both a desktop GUI and a CLI, unlike many backup tools that are CLI-only
Deduplication and compression reduce storage costs for cloud backup destinations
Cross-platform support for Windows, macOS, and Linux
Active community support forum
cons: No official managed/hosted backup service — users must supply their own storage destination
No published enterprise support plan or paid tier

faqs.question: Is Kopia free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kopia is free, open-source backup software.

faqs.question: Does Kopia have a graphical interface?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kopia offers both a desktop GUI application and a command-line interface.

faqs.question: What platforms does Kopia support?
faqs.answer: Kopia supports Windows, macOS, and Linux.

faqs.question: Does Kopia deduplicate backups?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kopia creates encrypted, compressed, and deduplicated backups to reduce storage usage.

use_cases.title: Encrypted cloud backups
use_cases.description: Back up laptops or servers to cloud storage of your choice with encryption, compression, and deduplication.

use_cases.title: Policy-driven backup management
use_cases.description: Define retention and scheduling policies to automate backups across multiple machines.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Kopia?
paragraphs: Kopia is a free, open-source backup tool that creates encrypted, compressed, and deduplicated backups to a cloud or local storage destination of the user's choice. It is designed to scale from individual laptops to large servers.
Unlike many backup tools that are command-line only, Kopia offers both a desktop graphical application and a CLI, along with policy-based rules for controlling backup behavior and retention.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Kopia is for
paragraphs: Kopia suits individuals and system administrators who want a free backup tool with both a user-friendly GUI and a scriptable CLI, running across Windows, macOS, and Linux.
